Chicago Public Schools (CPS) responds to all public records requests in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq. 
Due to the volume and complexity of requests received in the normal course of business, CPS is extending the time for its initial response to your request by an additional 5 business days (to 10 business days total beginning on the first business day following receipt of the request) pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(e)(v) which allows for the examination and evaluation of the requested records by personnel to determine if they are exempt from disclosure under Section 7 of Illinois FOIA or should be revealed only with appropriate deletions. 
Upon the completion of a response, CPS will provide the first 50 pages to you at no charge.  If a response is more than 50 pages, a fee of 15¢ per page will be charged for each additional page. All applicable fees must be paid prior to the copying. CPS will inform you of any fees due if your paper response exceeds 50 pages. You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Thank you for using the CPS FOIA Center.

title: BWC & Incident Records: Jeremy Reynolds Multi-County Pursuit, Stolen Cruiser & Standoff – Richland County, OH (March 2022)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Ohio Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am submitting this request on behalf of my professional YouTube news and educational channel that reports on police interactions, body camera footage, public safety incidents, and transparency in law enforcement. Our content is journalistic in nature, aimed at informing the public about police procedures and accountability through factual, narrated analysis of public records.
 
I am requesting all available body-worn camera (BWC) and dashcam footage, as well as the initial incident report, regarding the multi-county pursuit, standoff, and stolen cruiser incident involving suspect Jeremy Reynolds.
 
Specific details to assist your search:
 
Incident Date: March 6, 2022
Location: Richland County, Ohio — Millsboro Road / Lexington-Springmill Road area; U.S. 30; State Route 511
Suspect Name: Jeremy Reynolds, 31
Agencies Involved: Ontario Police Department, Ohio State Highway Patrol
 
Incident Summary: Troopers attempted to stop a suspected stolen vehicle driven by Reynolds. He struck several vehicles during the pursuit, causing his car to break down on Millsboro Road. A 40-minute standoff ensued. Reynolds then escaped using smoke from the disabled vehicle as cover, stole an unlocked Ohio State Highway Patrol cruiser — with a woman still in the back seat — and drove off despite being shot twice. He later abandoned the cruiser, carjacked a minivan, and led officers on a second chase until troopers intentionally rammed the minivan to end the pursuit.
 
Records requested:
 
All BWC footage from Ontario PD officers and Ohio State Highway Patrol troopers involved in the initial pursuit, standoff, shooting, and second pursuit.
 
All dashcam and in-car camera footage from all patrol vehicles involved.
 
The complete incident and arrest reports, including all officer narratives.
 
All 911 call recordings and dispatch/CAD logs related to this incident.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Marija Malesevic

title: BWC & Incident Records: Sophia Ross DUI Arrest – Sarasota, FL (November 2024)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am submitting this request on behalf of my professional YouTube news and educational channel that reports on police interactions, body camera footage, public safety incidents, and transparency in law enforcement. Our content is journalistic in nature, aimed at informing the public about police procedures and accountability through factual, narrated analysis of public records.
 
I am requesting all available body-worn camera (BWC) and dashcam footage, as well as the initial incident report, regarding the DUI arrest of Sophia Ross.
 
Specific details to assist your search:
 
Incident Date: November 17, 2024
Location: Sarasota, Florida
Suspect Name: Sophia Ross, 22
Arresting Agency: Sarasota Police Department
 
Incident Summary: Officers pulled Ross over after observing her stopping at green lights and striking several curbs. Upon contact, Ross was swaying off balance, slurring her words, and acting lethargic. She admitted to having been drinking at a bar. During the traffic stop, Ross repeatedly called the officer "babe" and "daddy," attempted to kiss the officer, flashed her Hooters uniform top, and tried to flirt her way out of the arrest. She initially refused field sobriety tests saying "I'm literally drinking and not gonna drive" and denied she had been driving. She was arrested and charged with DUI. Her blood alcohol content was 0.318, nearly four times the legal limit.
 
Records requested:
 
All BWC footage from all officers who responded to and interacted with Sophia Ross during the traffic stop, field sobriety testing, arrest, and transport.
 
All dashcam footage from patrol vehicles present during the stop.
 
The complete incident and arrest report, including all officer narratives.
 
All dispatch/CAD logs related to this traffic stop.
 
Any booking or intake video/audio recordings from the jail.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

title: BWC & Incident Records: Federal Judge Thomas Ludington DUI Arrest – Emmet County, MI (October 2025)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Michigan Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am submitting this request on behalf of my professional YouTube news and educational channel that reports on police interactions, body camera footage, public safety incidents, and transparency in law enforcement. Our content is journalistic in nature, aimed at informing the public about police procedures and accountability through factual, narrated analysis of public records.
 
I am requesting all available body-worn camera (BWC) and dashcam footage, as well as the initial incident report, regarding the DUI arrest of U.S. District Judge Thomas Ludington following a single-vehicle crash.
 
Specific details to assist your search:
 
Incident Date: October 2025
Location: Emmet County, Michigan
Suspect Name: U.S. District Judge Thomas Ludington, 72
Arresting Agency: Michigan State Police
 
Incident Summary: Judge Ludington, a George W. Bush appointee to the federal bench, crashed his Cadillac and was arrested for operating while intoxicated. His blood alcohol content was 0.27 — more than three times the legal limit of 0.08. Bodycam footage shows Ludington slurring his words, denying having anything to drink, claiming he didn't know how his car crashed or why his airbags deployed, and having difficulty following police instructions. He was charged with OWI and OWI with a BAC of .17 or higher.
 
Records requested:
 
All BWC footage from all Michigan State Police troopers who responded to the crash and interacted with Judge Ludington during the investigation, field sobriety testing, arrest, and transport.
 
All dashcam footage from patrol vehicles at the scene.
 
The complete incident and arrest report, including all officer narratives.
 
All 911 call recordings and dispatch/CAD logs related to this incident.
 
Any booking or intake video/audio recordings.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Marija Malesevic

title: BWC & Incident Records: Tiger Woods DUI Arrest – Jupiter, FL (May 2017)

communications.communication:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Incident Date: May 29, 2017
Location: Military Trail, Jupiter, Palm Beach County, Florida
Suspect Name: Eldrick Tont "Tiger" Woods

Officers had to wake him. Woods appeared disoriented and failed field sobriety tests. He was arrested for DUI. A breathalyzer showed 0.000 BAC, but toxicology later revealed five prescription drugs in his system including Vicodin, Dilaudid, Xanax, Ambien, and THC. He later pled guilty to reckless driving.
 
Records requested:
 
All BWC footage from responding and arresting officers who interacted with Tiger Woods during the traffic encounter, field sobriety testing, arrest, and transport.
 
All dashcam footage from patrol vehicles present at the scene.
 
The complete incident and arrest report, including all officer narratives.
 
All 911 call recordings and dispatch/CAD logs related to this incident.
 
Any booking or intake video/audio recordings from the Palm Beach County Jail.

communications.communication:

communications.communication:
Good morning, Mr. Williams.

In your April 8, 2026 message to the Baltimore City Department of Public Works (“DPW”), you requested records related to holiday lighting and seasonal decorative lighting services by the city.  The Maryland Public Information Act (“PIA”), codified in §§ 4-101 through 4-601 of the General Provisions Article (“GP”) of the Maryland Code governs your request.

This response is on behalf of DPW.  After review, DPW has determined that it has no records that are responsive to your request. Each Baltimore City agency is the custodian of its own records. See Baltimore City Code, Article 1 § 10-1. If responsive records exist, they may be in the custody and control of the Department of Finance’s Bureau of Procurement. For a complete list of agency custodians, please see https://oag.maryland.gov/resources-info/Documents/pdfs/Appendix_J.pdf

Nothing in this response is intended to indicate that any records sought from City agencies exist or to waive any privileges held by the Mayor and City Council.  You may contest this response by filing a complaint in Circuit Court pursuant to GP § 4-362.

Regards,

Dominic

communications.subject: Records Request Confirmation
communications.communication:
Dear Lia Jones, 
  
 Thank you for submitting a request for public information. This is your Request Number: 2026-102-MPIA and your security key is EB1B765A. Please retain this information, as it will be required when communicating with the City Clerk regarding your request. 
  
 Pursuant to the Maryland Public Information Act, the City will provide an initial response within 10 working days of receipt of your request. If additional time is required to complete the request, you will be notified accordingly. If you did not provide contact information at the time of submission, you are responsible for contacting the City Clerk’s Office to confirm receipt and check the status of your request. In all communications or when picking up records, please reference your request number and security key. 
  
 Md. Code, Gen. Prov. Art., § 4-206 permits the City to charge reasonable fees for the search for and response to a Public Information Act request. The City charges a fee equivalent to the employee working on the request’s hourly pay times the number of hours worked. The first two hours are free. 
  
 If you have any questions or need assistance, please contact: Lia Jones, City Clerk, Phone: 240-805-1084, Email: cityclerk@gaithersburgmd.gov.  You may also use this system to upload any additional documents related to your request.

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: CPRA - Ventura County - Flock Safety Documents (Ventura Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

- Organization Audit in Flock. 
All data logged from July 1, 2023 to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, this report is available within the Insights tab and reflects searches conducted within the agency.

- Network Audit in Flock. 
All data logged from July 1, 2023 to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, this report is available within the Insights tab and reflects searches of the agency's Flock network by any agency in the Flock system.

- Hardware.
The current number and installed locations of any Flock Safety, LPR,  AVIS, or machine learning-assisted camera systems (provide locations as coordinates or address with nearest cross-street), and any documents related to planned future installations of the same.

- Documents.
>  Invoices, contracts, purchase orders, and bid documents related to the acquisition or renewal of Flock Safety products or services
>  Agreements with homeowner associations, private businesses, or individuals for access to Flock Safety systems
>  Training materials, policies, and SOPs related to Flock Safety platform use
>  Grant or funding records related to the Flock system
>  Data retention policies for data collected by Flock Safety systems
>  Any complaints or grievances received from the public regarding ALPR camera placement or data use


I am not requesting personally identifiable information or license plate data. If any responsive record contains such information, please redact only that portion and produce the remainder. If any records are withheld, please provide a written index identifying each withheld record and the statutory exemption claimed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jordan

communications.communication:

This include medical encounter records, medical intake screenings, sick call requests, treatment notes, and any other medical or health-related documentation pertaining to Mr. Garcia-Aviles during his detention.

Proof of death is not required, as Garcia-Aviles' death has been widely reported, including in an ICE press release: https://www.ice.gov/news/releases/illegal-alien-dies-victorville-medical-center-california-after-complications-alcohol
